description: SC category female, rural area, service sector — small tailoring & beauty parlour unit; tests special-category × rural = 35% subsidy and 5% own contribution for women-owned SC enterprise

business.name: Sri Lakshmi Tailoring & Beauty Parlour
business.description: Tailoring unit with beauty parlour services for rural households

notes: Hand-verified against PMEGP scheme rules + src/engines/financial-engine/index.ts and src/engines/eligibility-engine/index.ts (engine-verified by running computeFinancials + checkEligibility). Subsidy rate 35% from src/knowledge-package/data/pmegp_subsidy_matrix.json: SPECIAL category (SC) + RURAL area → subsidyRate 35, ownContributionPercent 5, maxProjectCost 5000000. isSpecialCategory() returns true because applicant.category === 'SC' (and applicant.isWomen === true would also trigger it independently). Means-of-finance algebra (engine lines 213-227): bankFinance = round((TPC - OC) / (1 + rate/100)) = round(760000 / 1.35) = 562963; subsidyAmount = TPC - OC - bankFinance = 800000 - 40000 - 562963 = 197037 (residual, avoids rounding drift). bankWorkingCapital = financials.workingCapital = 400000; bankTermLoan = max(0, bankFinance - workingCapital) = 562963 - 400000 = 162963. EMI via reducing-balance formula (P=162963, r=0.11/12, n=84) = 2790. Break-even %: fixedCosts = monthlyOverheads*12 + depreciation = 25000*12 + (200000/10) = 320000; variableCosts = rawMaterials*12 = 180000; contribution = annualRev - variableCosts = 900000 - 180000 = 720000; breakEvenPercent = 320000/720000*100 = 44.44%. DSCR: annualNetProfit = 900000 - (70000*12 + 20000) = 40000; first-12-month debt service after moratorium ≈ 12 * EMI = 33480 (interest-heavy initially); DSCR = (NP + depreciation)/debtService = (40000 + 20000)/33480 = 1.79. Eligibility: all 7 engine checks pass (age.min 28>=18, age.max 28<=65, negative-list empty, cost.ceiling 8L <= 25L SERVICE, no prior subsidy, INDIVIDUAL permitted, education GRADUATE OK for cost <= 10L). Note: matrix description says 'rates are percentages of bank finance' but engine implements algebraic subsidy as residual (engine lines 213-227), producing the subsidyAmount above rather than a literal bankFinance × rate / 100 calculation — this fixture records engine behavior, not the matrix's literal description. breakEvenPercent and dscr are rounded to 2 decimals; the engine emits raw floats (44.44444444444444 and 1.7921146953405018) — a strict regression test should round engine output to 2 decimals before comparing.
